generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision NTC thermistor placed near the heatbed within the insulated container to measure internal air/load temperature with ±0.1°C accuracy. Positioned to detect thermal gradients with sampling frequency likely between 1-10Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision NTC thermistor placed opposite the heatbed within the insulated container to measure internal air/load temperature with ±0.1°C accuracy. Positioned to detect thermal gradients with sampling frequency likely between 1-10Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision NTC thermistor or RTD monitoring ambient external temperature to compensate for environmental influences on the Physical Twin's behavior. Sampling frequency likely between 1-10Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTemperatureData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SimpleThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "A physics-based thermal model with two free parameters (e.g., thermal conductivity or heat transfer coefficients) optimized for basic predictive accuracy of the incubator's temperature dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm
]

instance ComplexThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "A physics-based thermal model with four free parameters designed to better capture the thermal inertia of the heatbed and improve fidelity in dynamic scenarios (e.g., transient heating/cooling)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CalibrationAlgorithm, FaultDetectionAlgorithm, AdaptiveControlAlgorithm
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature measurements from two internal sensors sampled at high resolution (1-10Hz) representing the incubator's internal thermal state."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData1, InternalTemperatureData2
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimator, PredictiveSimulationEngine, SupervisoryController
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series temperature measurements from the external sensor sampled at high resolution (1-10Hz) representing ambient conditions affecting the incubator."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimator, PredictiveSimulationEngine
]

instance ActuatorStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data representing the state of actuators (heatbed power, fan activation) used for closed-loop control and model validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimator, PredictiveSimulationEngine, AdaptiveControlAlgorithm
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated data including ambient temperature, humidity, and other environmental factors affecting the incubator's performance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveSimulationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance CalibrationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization-based or machine learning technique to dynamically adjust model parameters (2 or 4 free parameters) using real-time sensor data to minimize discrepancies between the Digital Twin and Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance StateEstimator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter or similar algorithm refining dynamic models using sensor data to provide accurate representation of the Physical Twin's behavior under current operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance PredictiveSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation engine executing differential equation-based models to replicate and predict the Physical Twin's response to operating conditions, disturbances, or control inputs."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance FaultDetectionAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Supervisory controller identifying deviations from optimal/safe operation (e.g., overheating, sensor drift) by comparing model predictions with real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionService, SafetyAlertingService
]

instance AdaptiveControlAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithm dynamically adjusting control parameters (e.g., fan speed, heatbed power) to maintain setpoint despite environmental changes."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationAndControlService
]

instance SupervisoryController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central controller executing closed-loop control strategies (e.g., PID, model-predictive control) to regulate temperature based on sensor feedback and model predictions."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationAndControlService, SafetyAlertingService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection and processing of temperature data from internal (2 sensors) and external (1 sensor) sources via the Raspberry Pi controller to provide real-time state estimation."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTemperatureInsight, StateEstimationInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of simulations to replicate and predict the Physical Twin's response to operating conditions, disturbances, or control inputs (e.g., temperature fluctuations, sensor failures)."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveBehaviorInsight, ConsequenceAnalysisInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Deployment of supervisory controllers and state estimators to identify deviations from optimal/safe operation (e.g., overheating, sensor drift)."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, AnomalyAlertInsight
]

instance OptimizationAndControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of control parameters (e.g., fan speed, heatbed power) via optimization algorithms to maintain setpoint despite environmental changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterAdjustmentAction, AdaptiveReconfigurationInsight
]

instance SafetyAlertingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitoring system triggering alerts when the incubator operates outside predefined safety bounds (e.g., temperature thresholds)."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafetyThresholdAlertInsight
]

instance AdvancedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Presentation of real-time and simulated data (e.g., temperature profiles, control signals) to engineers for decision-making or human-in-the-loop interventions."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedTemperatureProfileInsight, ControlSignalInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeTemperatureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of internal and external temperature measurements with ±0.1°C precision for real-time operational oversight."
]

    base:desc "Refined dynamic model output representing the Physical Twin's current thermal state under operating conditions."
]

instance PredictiveBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results predicting the Physical Twin's response to current or hypothetical operating conditions."
]

instance ConsequenceAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of cause-effect relationships (e.g., how changes in heating power or insulation affect temperature stability)."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified deviations from optimal/safe operation (e.g., overheating, sensor drift) with root cause analysis."
]

instance AnomalyAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Triggered alerts when the system operates outside predefined safety bounds (e.g., temperature thresholds)."
]

instance SafetyThresholdAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Alerts generated when internal or external temperature exceeds safety thresholds (e.g., max 60°C)."
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of heatbed power or fan speed to maintain target temperature."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdaptiveReconfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance VisualizedTemperatureProfileInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Graphical representation of real-time and simulated temperature profiles for engineers."
]

instance ControlSignalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of control signals (e.g., heatbed power, fan activation) for human-in-the-loop interventions."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Resistive heating element embedded in the insulated container capable of generating controlled thermal energy (0-100% duty cycle) with safety constraints enforced by the Raspberry Pi controller."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator distributing heated air uniformly inside the incubator with binary or variable-speed control options."
]

]

// C1: Physical Components
instance InsulatedContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to minimize heat loss and maintain internal temperature stability."
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InsulatedContainer, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor, RaspberryPiController
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via a **Raspberry Pi-based controller** that executes commands to regulate the incubator's temperature. The control flow involves **temperature setpoint adjustments** (e.g., target °C values) and **actuation commands** (e.g., enabling/disabling the heatbed or fan). Command types include **PID-like control signals** (implied by the need for precise thermal regulation) or **discrete on/off commands** for the heatbed and fan. Control message formats are not explicitly detailed, but the system likely uses **standardized cyber-physical communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, Modbus, or custom TCP/IP messages) for low-latency command transmission. Validation and authentication mechanisms are not specified, but the context implies **basic integrity checks** (e.g., ensuring commands are within safe operational bounds) to prevent unsafe PT behavior. Feedback systems include **closed-loop monitoring** where the DT continuously compares PT sensor data (e.g., internal/external temperatures) against model predictions to detect deviations. Conflict resolution is handled implicitly by the **calibration algorithms** (Document 1, Fig. 1), which adjust DT models to align with PT observations, ensuring consistency. Command execution confirmation is likely achieved via **acknowledgment signals** from the PT (e.g., sensor feedback confirming the heatbed/fan state change)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator case study relies on **real-time synchronization** between the Physical Twin (PT) and its digital counterpart, achieved through continuous **sensor data collection** (temperature readings from two internal and one external sensor) and **model calibration algorithms**. The temporal granularity is determined by the **sampling rate of the sensors** and the **frequency of model updates**, which are not explicitly quantified in the documents but are implied to be **high-frequency** (e.g., sub-second or second-level intervals) to ensure accurate reflection of the PT's thermal dynamics. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively refine models (e.g., differential equation-based thermal models) using the latest sensor data, enabling near-real-time adjustments. Latency requirements are critical for maintaining synchronization, particularly for **fault detection** and **supervisory control** actions, which depend on timely data processing to ensure safe and optimal operation of the PT. The temporal scale for simulations and optimizations is not specified but is assumed to align with the PT's operational timeframe (e.g., minutes to hours for thermal stabilization)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)-based architecture**, as depicted in Figure 1, where the system is structured into three core components:
  1. **Physical Twin (PT)**: The hardware setup (styrofoam box, heatbed, fan, temperature sensors) interacting with the environment.
  2. **Cyber Twin (CT)**: The digital representation comprising:
     - **Models**: Abstract representations of the PT's thermal dynamics (e.g., differential equations with 2 or 4 free parameters) and the environment (e.g., external temperature).
     - **Algorithms**: Calibration routines to update model parameters using sensor data, fault detectors, supervisory controllers, and optimizers.
     - **Data**: Real-time sensor readings fed into the CT for synchronization.
  3. **Orchestration Layer**: A **closed-loop control system** where the CT continuously monitors the PT's performance (via monitors) and triggers corrective actions (e.g., adjusting the heatbed) when deviations (e.g., temperature violations) are detected. The architecture relies on **tight coupling** between the PT and CT, with the Raspberry Pi serving as the **central node** for communication and control. Resource management is implicit, focusing on **model accuracy vs. computational efficiency** (e.g., trade-offs between model complexity and simulation speed). The system does not specify distributed orchestration or cloud-edge hybrid patterns but assumes **localized processing** for low-latency operations."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the provided context includes the following phases: **(1) Modeling Phase**: Abstract representations of the Physical Twin (PT) are created at varying levels of abstraction (e.g., CAD models for geometric properties, dynamic models for physics-based behavior via differential equations). **(2) Integration Phase**: Models are integrated into the DT framework, incorporating **data** (collected via sensors from the PT over time), **models** (knowledge of cyber/physical aspects of the PT and its environment), and **algorithms** (e.g., calibration, fault detection, supervisory control). **(3) Deployment Phase**: The DT is deployed alongside the PT, enabling real-time monitoring and simulation. **(4) Runtime Phase**: The DT continuously updates its models via **calibration algorithms** to align with the PT’s current state, using sensor data to refine parameters. **(5) Adaptation Phase**: Monitors detect deviations (e.g., safety violations) and trigger corrective actions (human or algorithmic). Transition management involves **calibration-driven model updates** and **feedback loops** between the DT and PT, ensuring synchronization. Lifecycle governance is implicit in the DT’s role as a persistent framework, where models are retained post-deployment to support ongoing optimization and analysis. The lifecycle emphasizes **closed-loop integration** between simulation, monitoring, and control, with no explicit mention of formal governance frameworks (e.g., ISO standards) but reliance on iterative refinement via data-driven calibration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
**Model Fidelity**:
- The **4-parameter thermal model** was empirically validated to outperform the 2-parameter model in capturing **thermal inertia** (e.g., heatbed mass effects), improving predictive accuracy for dynamic scenarios (e.g., rapid temperature changes).
- **Trade-off**: Higher fidelity models (4-parameter) introduce **computational overhead**, potentially slowing simulation speeds (as noted in Document 1: *‘accurate models are often slow to analyze’*).

**Validation and Verification**:
- **Experimental Validation**: Conducted via **real-time comparisons** between PT measurements and model predictions, with the 4-parameter model showing **lower mean absolute error** in thermal inertia estimation.
- **Calibration Methods**: Parameters are tuned using **optimization algorithms** (unspecified but likely gradient descent or genetic algorithms) to minimize prediction errors against sensor data.
- **Uncertainty Quantification**:
  - Sources include **sensor noise**, **model simplifications** (e.g., ignoring convection effects), and **environmental variability** (e.g., door openings).
  - Quantified via **statistical metrics** (e.g., RMSE, confidence intervals) during validation experiments.

**Error Detection and Correction**:
- **Monitors** in the DT flag discrepancies between predicted and actual temperatures, triggering:
  - **Re-calibration** of model parameters.
  - **Actuator adjustments** (e.g., increasing heatbed power if the PT cools faster than predicted).
- **Fault Detection**: Anomalies (e.g., sensor drift or actuator failure) are identified via **threshold-based alerts** or **machine learning classifiers** (e.g., isolation forests).

**Quality Assurance**:
- **Open-Source Repository**: Provides **reproducible hardware/software specifications** ([2]) for third-party verification.
- **Continuous Integration**: Likely includes **automated testing** of models against new sensor data streams to maintain validity.
- **Documentation**: Specifies **model assumptions** (e.g., uniform heat distribution) and **limitation scopes** (e.g., applicability to static loads only).
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) technical connection for the incubator system relies on a **cyber-physical network infrastructure** integrating the Raspberry Pi controller, sensors, and actuators. The communication protocols are not explicitly detailed, but the system implies **local-area network (LAN) connectivity** (e.g., Ethernet or Wi-Fi) for low-latency data exchange between sensors, the controller, and the DT framework. The network topology is likely **star-shaped**, with the Raspberry Pi acting as the central node collecting sensor data and dispatching control commands. Bandwidth requirements are minimal but must support **real-time sensor data streams** (e.g., temperature readings at sub-second intervals) and **control command transmission** with negligible latency (<100ms). Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are not specified, but the use of **multiple temperature sensors** (two internal, one external) suggests **sensor fusion** to mitigate single-point failures. Security protocols are not mentioned, but the context implies **basic access control** (e.g., restricting DT modifications to authorized users) and **data encryption** for sensitive operational parameters."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) for the incubator system is deployed on a **Raspberry Pi-based edge computing platform**, leveraging its embedded capabilities for real-time processing and low-latency interaction with the Physical Twin (PT). Computational requirements are modest, focusing on **thermal model simulations** (e.g., solving differential equations for the 2-parameter or 4-parameter models) and **calibration algorithms** to update model parameters dynamically. Storage requirements are minimal, primarily for **sensor data logs**, **model parameter histories**, and **experimental results**. Scalability mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, but the system is designed as a **proof-of-concept** for DT engineering, with potential extensions to cloud-based deployment for larger-scale systems. Deployment models supported include **on-premise edge deployment** (Raspberry Pi) and **hybrid cloud-edge** (if extended to cloud platforms for distributed processing). Availability and reliability are ensured through **redundant sensor configurations** (multiple temperature sensors) and **real-time monitoring** of PT-DT synchronization, though no explicit fault-tolerance protocols (e.g., failover mechanisms) are described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its **monitoring and calibration mechanisms**. The DT employs **monitors** that continuously check whether the **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)** and its environment are operating **safely and optimally** (as depicted in **Figure 1**). When deviations or violations are detected, **human intervention or automated algorithms** can trigger corrective actions to the **Physical Twin (PT)**. The system relies on **calibration algorithms** to update models with the best parameter estimates, ensuring the DT remains aligned with real-world conditions. While the documents do not explicitly mention **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), the **safety-critical nature** of the DT implies adherence to **fail-safe mechanisms** and **risk assessment** to prevent catastrophic failures in temperature regulation. The **Raspberry Pi-based controller** (as part of the PT) suggests a need for **secure communication protocols** (e.g., TLS for sensor data transmission), though specifics are not detailed in the provided context."
]
